talk_metadata.session_title: Advances in Hemologic Malignancies: Population Sciences for Hematological Malignancies
talk_metadata.talk_title: Infrastructure for Diversity-Enabled Genomic Research at Roswell Park
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have an 80% plus accrual rate, so it's successful"
Speaker said: "we are still figuring it out... we don't know the answer yet"
Speaker said: "we have 1.3 million blood, bone marrow, and other sample types from over 50,000 patients"

key_data_gaps: No efficacy data for the infrastructure itself

crispro_opportunity.description: Roswell Park's integrated data commons and universal consent model could be enhanced with CrisPRO's computational spine to better stratify patients by genetic ancestry and exposure data.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we have an integrated data commons with genomic data, clinical data, and that's highly searchable"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can overlay ancestry-informed risk models onto the searchable data commons to identify hidden subpopulations for targeted studies.

unresolved_questions: How will the transition to e-consent affect enrollment rates?
What statistical methods will be used to control for ancestry and exposure confounders in future analyses?

talk_metadata.session_title: Advances in Hemologic Malignancies: Population Sciences for Hematological Malignancies
talk_metadata.talk_title: Genetic Ancestry and Outcome Disparities in AML
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Black patients have an almost 12-year shorter overall survival"
Speaker said: "NPM1 mutation is the most adverse risk predictor in African American patients"
Speaker said: "African ancestry patients have global hypermethylation associated with immune response patterns"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Ancestry-specific transcriptional and epigenetic context altering NPM1 mutation impact
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "African ancestry NPM1 mutated patients have a very different profile"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker said: "Patients with NPM1 mutation in African ancestry have almost all relapsed"

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: ALLIANCE cooperative study group AML trial
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Standard AML chemotherapy efficacy across ancestries
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all-comers without ancestry stratification leading to diluted efficacy signals

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker revealed ELN risk predictions perform poorly in non-European ancestries, indicating limited generalizability
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Applying European-derived risk models to diverse populations dilutes predictive power

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that NPM1 mutation is a favorable risk marker in European ancestry AML, then concluded that the same mutation is the most adverse risk predictor in African American patients — ignoring the ancestry-specific biology.

key_data_gaps: Limited number of African ancestry patients in existing trials
Lack of prospective validation of ancestry-adjusted risk models

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop ancestry-adjusted prognostic models for NPM1 and other mutations using CrisPRO's computational framework.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "NPM1 mutation is the most adverse risk predictor in African American patients"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate genomic, epigenomic, and transcriptomic data with ancestry to recalibrate risk scores.

crispro_opportunity.description: Target the hypermethylation and immune response pathways identified in African ancestry AML patients.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "African ancestry patients have global hypermethylation associated with immune response patterns"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can prioritize epigenetic drugs for patients with the identified methylation signature.

cited_competitors.name: ELN risk classification
cited_competitors.context: Used as standard prognostic tool but shown to be less accurate in non-European patients

cited_competitors.name: Beat AML
cited_competitors.context: Provided comparative mutation frequency data for African ancestry cohorts

unresolved_questions: What specific molecular mechanisms drive the adverse impact of NPM1 mutations in African ancestry patients?
Can epigenetic modifiers reverse the hypermethylation phenotype observed?

talk_metadata.session_title: Advances in Hemologic Malignancies: Population Sciences for Hematological Malignancies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We see a large amount of this admixed American population where people that identify as Hispanic or Latino would be"
Speaker said: "CRLF2 rearrangement is present in ~80% of Hispanic PH-like ALL cases"
Speaker said: "AID motifs cluster at translocation breakpoints, suggesting a mechanistic driver"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: AID-mediated off-target breaks not addressed by current therapies
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker noted that a subset of Hispanic patients respond poorly despite CRLF2 targeting

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Targeted JAK2/CRLF2 inhibition in PH-like ALL
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Lack of ancestry-informed stratification leading to mixed response cohorts
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Genetic ancestry and AID activity signatures
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small sample size for Hispanic subgroup

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Current assays do not capture AID-driven mutational signatures that may predict resistance
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad enrollment without ancestry or AID activity biomarkers dilutes therapeutic signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that AID motifs drive CRLF2 translocations, then concluded that CRLF2 overexpression alone is sufficient for disease classification, ignoring the upstream AID mechanism.

key_data_gaps: Limited functional validation of AID's role in Hispanic patients
No long-term outcome data for targeted therapies in this subgroup

crispro_opportunity.description: Pair AID inhibition strategies with CRLF2/JAK2 targeted therapies for Hispanic PH-like ALL.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "AID motifs cluster at translocation breakpoints, suggesting a mechanistic driver"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic effects of AID inhibition with existing drugs to prioritize combos.

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop ancestry-informed digital PCR assays to detect AID activity at CRLF2 loci.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We developed an innovative digital PCR assay to interrogate the sequences of CRLF2"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate dPCR readouts with ancestry data to define high-risk subcohorts.

cited_competitors.name: Michael Lieber's lab
cited_competitors.context: Provided evidence that AID motifs are enriched at translocation breakpoints

cited_competitors.name: MD Anderson PH-like ALL cohort
cited_competitors.context: Reported 70% Hispanic/Latino representation in PH-like ALL cases

unresolved_questions: What is the precise contribution of AID expression levels to CRLF2 translocation frequency in Hispanic patients?
Can JAK2 R683G mutation be therapeutically targeted in the context of specific ancestry backgrounds?
